enrichment.short_description: No-code platform for building web apps, client portals, and internal tools on top of Airtable, Google Sheets, or Softr's own database.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Softr is a no-code platform founded in 2019 by Mariam Hakobyan and Artur Mkrtchyan and headquartered in Berlin, Germany. It lets non-technical builders turn structured data into fully functional web apps, client portals, marketplaces, and internal tools. Rather than starting from a blank canvas, Softr treats an existing dataset in Airtable, Google Sheets, or its own built-in database as the source of truth, then layers a visual interface, logic, and permissions on top of it.

The platform is organized around pre-built blocks, such as lists, tables, forms, charts, and calendars, along with full application templates covering common use cases like directories, dashboards, and membership sites. Builders assemble pages by dragging blocks onto a canvas, binding them to records, and configuring user groups, workflows, and payment rules through point-and-click settings rather than code, with optional custom CSS and JavaScript available for deeper tweaks.

Softr competes in the broader no-code app-building category alongside tools such as Bubble, Glide, and Stacker, but differentiates itself by leaning heavily on spreadsheet-like data sources that many small businesses already maintain. That positioning has helped it attract solo founders, agencies, and internal ops teams who want to ship a working app quickly without hiring engineers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Softr Is For
paragraphs: Softr suits non-technical founders and solopreneurs who already keep their business data in Airtable or Google Sheets and want a client-facing or internal front end without commissioning custom software. It works well for anyone whose core need is displaying, filtering, and editing records behind a login rather than building a novel algorithm or interaction pattern.

Agencies and consultants are a natural fit too, since Softr's white-label options, custom domains, and user-group permissions make it practical to spin up branded client portals for multiple customers from a shared template, then customize each instance quickly.

Internal operations, HR, and support teams also use Softr to build lightweight internal tools, such as employee directories, approval trackers, or support dashboards, where the priority is getting a usable interface in front of colleagues fast rather than engineering a polished consumer product.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Teams that need a highly bespoke user interface, an unconventional interaction design, or business logic that goes well beyond filtering, forms, and simple workflows will likely find the block-based builder confining, since deep customization usually requires injected custom code rather than native support.

Organizations expecting to operate at large scale, with very high record counts, heavy concurrent usage, or complex relational data models, may outgrow the practical limits of spreadsheet-style or hosted data sources and the app-user tiers Softr's plans are priced around, eventually needing a custom-built backend instead.

Anyone who specifically needs native iOS or Android apps distributed through an app store should also look elsewhere, since Softr publishes responsive web apps and progressive web apps rather than compiling native mobile binaries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At its core, Softr connects to Airtable, Google Sheets, or its own built-in database, then exposes that data through building blocks like lists, tables, grids, calendars, and charts, so builders can display and edit records without writing queries.

Access control is handled through user groups and permissions, letting builders restrict pages, records, or fields to specific customer segments, team roles, or paying subscribers, while workflows and automations trigger emails, record updates, and multi-step actions in response to user behavior.

Softr also bundles native Stripe-based payments for one-time charges and subscriptions, AI blocks for generating or summarizing content inside an app, custom code and custom domain support for white-labeling, and integrations with tools like Zapier and Make for connecting external systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Softr Experience
paragraphs: Getting started with Softr is generally fast: builders pick a template or start from a data source, and the drag-and-drop editor keeps most decisions visual, which shortens the path from idea to a shareable link compared with hand-coding a front end.

The trade-off shows up as projects grow more ambitious; achieving a fully custom look or intricate logic often means reaching for the custom code panel or a third-party integration, and heavier apps can feel less responsive than a purpose-built application.

Overall, Softr trades some ceiling on customization and performance for a substantially lower floor on time and technical skill required to launch, which is why it tends to earn strong marks from non-technical builders shipping portals and internal tools rather than teams building highly differentiated consumer products.

features.title: Multiple Data Sources
features.description: Connects to Airtable, Google Sheets, or Softr's own built-in database as the backend for any app.

features.title: Pre-built Blocks and Templates
features.description: A library of UI blocks (lists, tables, forms, charts, calendars) and full app templates for portals, marketplaces, and directories.

features.title: User Groups and Permissions
features.description: Role-based access control to show or hide pages, records, and fields for different customer or team segments.

features.title: Workflows and Automations
features.description: Point-and-click automations that send emails, update records, and chain multi-step actions without code.

features.title: AI Blocks
features.description: Built-in AI features for generating, summarizing, or transforming content directly inside a published app.

features.title: Payments
features.description: Native Stripe integration for accepting one-time payments and recurring subscriptions inside an app.

features.title: Custom Code and Domains
features.description: Ability to inject custom CSS and JavaScript and connect a custom domain for a fully white-labeled app.

features.title: Responsive and PWA Support
features.description: Apps are mobile-responsive by default and can be installed as progressive web apps on phones.

features.title: Third-Party Integrations
features.description: Connects to external tools through Zapier, Make, and native integrations to extend app functionality.

pros: Fast to launch functional web apps without writing code
Works directly with Airtable and Google Sheets data teams already use
Free plan and free trial let users test the platform before paying
Built-in payments, permissions, and automations reduce the need for extra tools
Template library speeds up common build patterns like portals and directories
cons: Paid plans grow more expensive as app users and data volume increase
No native iOS or Android app compilation, only responsive web and PWA output
Complex custom logic can hit the limits of the block-based builder
Performance can lag behind a custom-coded application at larger scale
Reliance on connected data sources means the underlying source's limits apply

faqs.question: Does Softr require coding skills?
faqs.answer: No. Softr is built as a no-code, drag-and-drop platform, though it offers optional custom code and JavaScript for advanced customization.

faqs.question: What data sources does Softr support?
faqs.answer: Softr can connect to Airtable, Google Sheets, or use its own built-in database as the backend for an app.

faqs.question: Can I accept payments in a Softr app?
faqs.answer: Yes. Softr includes a native Stripe integration for one-time payments and recurring subscriptions.

faqs.question: Does Softr offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. Softr has a free plan with limited app users and database records, alongside paid plans that raise those limits.

faqs.question: Can I use a custom domain with a Softr app?
faqs.answer: Yes. Custom domain support is available, letting builders publish apps under their own branded domain.

use_cases.title: Client Portals
use_cases.description: Agencies and service businesses build branded, permissioned portals so clients can view project data, submit requests, or track status.

use_cases.title: Internal Tools
use_cases.description: Operations, HR, and support teams build directories, trackers, and dashboards on top of existing spreadsheet or Airtable data.

use_cases.title: Marketplaces and Directories
use_cases.description: Founders build two-sided marketplaces or listing directories using Airtable records as the underlying data model.

use_cases.title: Membership and Community Sites
use_cases.description: Creators build gated content or community platforms with paid membership tiers powered by the built-in payments feature.
